dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3432 IS: Working Families Disaster Tax Relief Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Klobuchar (for herself and Mr. Cassidy) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to allow disaster-affected taxpayers to use preceding taxable year earned income to determine eligibility for the earned income credit and the refundable portion of the child tax credit.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Working Families Disaster Tax Relief Act.

section.header: Election for disaster-affected taxpayers to use preceding year earned income to determine credit eligibility

subsection.header: Election To use prior year income for disaster-Affected taxpayers with respect to child tax credit
subsection.text: Section 24(d) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended to read as follows:

paragraph.header: Election to use prior year income for disaster-affected taxpayers

subparagraph.text: A disaster-affected taxpayer may elect to apply paragraph (1) by substituting the preceding taxable year for the taxable year each place such term appears.
For purposes of this paragraph, the term disaster-affected taxpayer means, with respect to a taxable year—

clause.text: a taxpayer whose principal place of abode or principal place of work, during any period in the taxable year which is part of the incident period of a qualified disaster, is located in a qualified disaster zone, or

subclause.text: whose principal place of abode, during any period in the taxable year which is part of the incident period of a qualified disaster, is located in the qualified disaster area with respect to such qualified disaster, and
who is displaced from such principal place of abode during such taxable year as a result of such qualified disaster.

clause.text: The term qualified disaster means any disaster with respect to which a major disaster has been declared by the President under section 401 of the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act.

The term qualified disaster area means, with respect to any qualified disaster, the area with respect to which the major disaster was declared under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act.

The term qualified disaster zone means the portion of a qualified disaster area determined by the President to warrant individual or individual and public assistance from the Federal Government under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act.

subsection.header: Election To use prior year income for disaster-Affected taxpayers with respect to earned income credit
subsection.text: Section 32(c) of such Code is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.header: Election to use prior year income for disaster-affected taxpayers
paragraph.text: A disaster-affected taxpayer (as defined in section 24(d)(4)(B)) may elect to apply subsection (a)(1) by inserting preceding before taxable year in such paragraph.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall apply to taxable years beginning after December 31, 2024.
